source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
Loading and unloading are essential components of the logistics process. A company has designed belt conveyors as shown in Figures A and B. The speed of the conveyor is adjustable, allowing different speeds to be set for different types of goods. The length of the conveyor is also adjustable, enabling it to extend directly into trailers for operation. Additionally, the height of the conveyor can be adjusted, making it convenient for workers to directly handle goods located at higher positions in the cargo compartment.

1. If cargo $A$ (which can be considered as a particle) is at the same height as the conveyor, the schematic diagram of the conveying process is shown in Figure C. It is known that the length of the conveyor is $L_{1}=6\,\mathrm{m}$, and the conveyor moves clockwise at a speed of $v_{0}=2\,\mathrm{m/s}$. The kinetic friction coefficient between cargo $A$ and the conveyor is $\mu_{1}=0.2$. Cargo $A$ is released from rest at the left end of the conveyor. Find the acceleration of cargo $A$ when it is just placed on the conveyor and the time it takes to travel from the left end to the right end. (Take $g=10\,\mathrm{m/s}^{2}$.)
2. If the cargo $B$ (which can be considered as a point mass) is at a higher position, the schematic diagram of the conveying process is shown in Figure D. It is known that the length of the conveyor becomes $L_{2}=12.05\mathrm{m}$, the inclination angle $\theta=37^{\circ}$, the dynamic friction coefficient between cargo $B$ and the conveyor $\mu_{2}=0.5$, and cargo $B$ is released from rest at the left end of the conveyor, which still rotates clockwise. When the speed of cargo $B$ at the right end exceeds $7\mathrm{m/s}$, the cargo will be damaged due to collision. To ensure the safety of the cargo, what condition should the speed $v_{0}$ of the conveyor satisfy? ($\sin37^{\circ}=0.6$, $\cos37^{\circ}=0.8$, and $g$ is taken as $10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$)
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
$2\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$, $3.5\mathrm{s}$
Translate the following text into English. Keep all LaTeX formulas and physical expressions unchanged. Less than $1\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}$ Do not add any other content.

image_dependency: Question explicitly references Figures A, B, C, and D for the conveyor setups and conveying process.
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the sample is intended to rely on visual information.
The problem involves conveyor orientation/inclination and left/right endpoint geometry, which are commonly figure-dependent spatial details.
asset_registry_record shows 2 existing images with usable dimensions, so the visual assets are present.
multi_step: There are two physics subproblems, each requiring setup from conditions, force analysis, and kinematic reasoning rather than a single lookup.
Part 1 asks for both initial acceleration and travel time, implying at least multiple linked computation steps.
Part 2 asks for a safety condition on conveyor speed based on friction, incline, and terminal-speed threshold, which requires conditional reasoning.
The long question text and multi_solution_policy.should_push_multi_solution_agent=true are consistent with substantial reasoning depth.
verifiability: The expected answer is numeric/inequality-based ('2 m/s^2', '3.5 s', and a bound on v0), which is in principle checkable by physics derivation.
answer_type=set and answer_present=true support structured outcome checking.
registry_passed=true increases confidence that the sample is usable for evaluation.

normalization_record.normalized_question_text:
Loading and unloading are essential components of the logistics process. A company has designed belt conveyors as shown in Figures A and B. The speed of the conveyor is adjustable, allowing different speeds to be set for different types of goods. The length of the conveyor is also adjustable, enabling it to extend directly into trailers for operation. Additionally, the height of the conveyor can be adjusted, making it convenient for workers to directly handle goods located at higher positions in the cargo compartment.

1. If cargo $A$ (which can be considered as a particle) is at the same height as the conveyor, the schematic diagram of the conveying process is shown in Figure C. It is known that the length of the conveyor is $L_{1}=6\,\mathrm{m}$, and the conveyor moves clockwise at a speed of $v_{0}=2\,\mathrm{m/s}$. The kinetic friction coefficient between cargo $A$ and the conveyor is $\mu_{1}=0.2$. Cargo $A$ is released from rest at the left end of the conveyor. Find the acceleration of cargo $A$ when it is just placed on the conveyor and the time it takes to travel from the left end to the right end. (Take $g=10\,\mathrm{m/s}^{2}$.)
2. If the cargo $B$ (which can be considered as a point mass) is at a higher position, the schematic diagram of the conveying process is shown in Figure D. When the speed of cargo $B$ at the right end exceeds $7\mathrm{m/s}$, the cargo will be damaged due to collision. To ensure the safety of the cargo, what condition should the speed $v_{0}$ of the conveyor satisfy? ($\sin37^{\circ}=0.6$, $\cos37^{\circ}=0.8$, and $g$ is taken as $10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$)

sentence_segments.text: Loading and unloading are essential components of the logistics process.
A company has designed belt conveyors as shown in Figures A and B.
The speed of the conveyor is adjustable, allowing different speeds to be set for different types of goods.
The length of the conveyor is also adjustable, enabling it to extend directly into trailers for operation.
Additionally, the height of the conveyor can be adjusted, making it convenient for workers to directly handle goods located at higher positions in the cargo compartment.
If cargo $A$ (which can be considered as a particle) is at the same height as the conveyor, the schematic diagram of the conveying process is shown in Figure C.
It is known that the length of the conveyor is $L_{1}=6\,\mathrm{m}$, and the conveyor moves clockwise at a speed of $v_{0}=2\,\mathrm{m/s}$.
The kinetic friction coefficient between cargo $A$ and the conveyor is $\mu_{1}=0.2$.
Cargo $A$ is released from rest at the left end of the conveyor.
Find the acceleration of cargo $A$ when it is just placed on the conveyor and the time it takes to travel from the left end to the right end.
(Take $g=10\,\mathrm{m/s}^{2}$.)
If the cargo $B$ (which can be considered as a point mass) is at a higher position, the schematic diagram of the conveying process is shown in Figure D.

It is known that the length of the conveyor becomes $L_{2}=12.05\mathrm{m}$, the inclination angle $\theta=37^{\circ}$, the dynamic friction coefficient between cargo $B$ and the conveyor $\mu_{2}=0.5$, and cargo $B$ is released from rest at the left end of the conveyor, which still rotates clockwise.
When the speed of cargo $B$ at the right end exceeds $7\mathrm{m/s}$, the cargo will be damaged due to collision.
To ensure the safety of the cargo, what condition should the speed $v_{0}$ of the conveyor satisfy?
($\sin37^{\circ}=0.6$, $\cos37^{\circ}=0.8$, and $g$ is taken as $10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$)

It is known that the length of the conveyor becomes $L_{2}=12.05\mathrm{m}$, the inclination angle $\theta=37^{\circ}$, the dynamic friction coefficient between cargo $B$ and the conveyor $\mu_{2}=0.5$, and cargo $B$ is released from rest at the left end of the conveyor, which still rotates clockwise. When the speed of cargo $B$ at the right end exceeds $7\mathrm{m/s}$, the cargo will be damaged due to collision. To ensure the safety of the cargo, what condition should the speed $v_{0}$ of the conveyor satisfy? ($\sin37^{\circ}=0.6$, $\cos37^{\circ}=0.8$, and $g$ is taken as $10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$)
